title: White Spaces - Experience

source.file: input_data/corpus/20200803 PC-Strategy-Solution Development and GoToMarket PlanVer1.5.xlsx

grain: one row per insurance value-chain function/stage
summary: A matrix template listing 20 insurance value-chain functions (rows) that was set up to be scored against segment and line-of-business columns (Personal / Commercial / Specialty, each split by Auto / Property / Liability / Worker's Comp). Only the value-chain function labels are populated; the grid body is entirely empty. In its current state it usefully enumerates the value-chain functions and the intended segment/LOB dimensions, but holds no white-space markings.
use_cases: What insurance value-chain functions/stages are enumerated (from Broker/Agency Management through Risk Control)?
What segment x line-of-business dimensions were intended for the white-space mapping?

columns.name: Value Chain
columns.note: value-chain function/stage; the only populated column

not_answerable.q: Which value-chain functions have white space in a given segment/line of business?
not_answerable.reason: the matrix body cells are all empty

not_answerable.q: What is the quantitative white-space score per segment?
not_answerable.reason: no data populated in the segment/LOB columns

warnings: two-row header band: row 2 segment groups (Personal/Commercial/Specialty) + row 3 line-of-business sub-columns (Auto/Property/Liability/Worker's Comp); header_row set to 2
matrix body entirely empty — only the 'Value Chain' row labels are populated; all segment/LOB columns are 100% null
